message:
fix(trace,channel,gen_server,h1,ws,io): LZ4 decompression bomb guard and turbofish type annotation fixes

src/trace/file.rs:
- Add decompression bomb guard in TraceReader and TraceEventIterator:
  check the LZ4 uncompressed-length prefix against MAX_COMPRESSED_CHUNK_LEN
  before calling decompress_size_prepended(), preventing OOM from
  maliciously crafted trace files with inflated length headers

src/channel/mpsc.rs:
- Add explicit turbofish SendError::<()> type annotations on all match
  arms in send(), try_send(), reserve(), try_reserve() and test assertions
  to satisfy nightly Rust type inference changes that require disambiguation
  when the error variant's generic parameter differs from the Ok path

src/channel/session.rs:
- Add explicit type annotations on TrackedSender::reserve() and
  tracked_send() return bindings for the same nightly inference reason

src/gen_server.rs:
- Add explicit Envelope<S> type annotations on all envelope construction
  sites (Call, Cast, Info) in GenServerHandle and GenServerRef
- Add Reply::<S::Reply> annotation in dispatch_envelope

src/http/h1/stream.rs:
- Add explicit Ok::<Frame<BytesCursor>, HttpError> turbofish on body
  channel send calls in IncomingBodyWriter and OutgoingBodySender

src/io/read_buf.rs:
- Change panic_message parameter from owned Box to &dyn Any + Send
  reference, avoiding unnecessary move; update call site to .as_ref()

src/net/websocket/split.rs:
- Add explicit ReuniteError::<IO> turbofish on error construction
- Use Self::initiate_close and Self::send_frame UFCS in WebSocketWriter
  to disambiguate method resolution

src/actor.rs:
- Use named format arguments (variants0, variants1, variants2) instead
  of positional indexing in format!() for clippy compliance

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
